{{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.

It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>

{{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers

{{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers

{{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of tumulus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Inside burial chamber File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-1.jpg|Southern standing monolith File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-2.jpg|Northern standing monolith 松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-1.JPG|Haniwa </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers

{{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of tumulus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Inside burial chamber File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-1.jpg|Southern standing monolith File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-2.jpg|Northern standing monolith 松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-1.JPG|Haniwa </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/?doc_id=5197 Kashiwara City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of tumulus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Inside burial chamber File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-1.jpg|Southern standing monolith File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-2.jpg|Northern standing monolith 松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-1.JPG|Haniwa </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|1=Matsuokayama Kofun|2=ja|3=松岳山古墳|4=fr|5=Kofun de Matsuokayama|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.

It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[:en:Jadeite|Jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun]][[en:Matsuokayama Kofun]][[fr:Kofun de Matsuokayama]][[ja:松岳山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11530088]] Wikidata name: Matsuokayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.569917|N|135.645722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun Group|qid=Q60986182}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|1=Matsuokayama Kofun|2=ja|3=松岳山古墳|4=fr|5=Kofun de Matsuokayama|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[:en:Jadeite|Jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[:en:Kawachi-Kokubu Station|Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of tumulus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Inside burial chamber File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-1.jpg|Southern standing monolith File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-2.jpg|Northern standing monolith 松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-1.JPG|Haniwa </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/?doc_id=5197 Kashiwara City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun]][[en:Matsuokayama Kofun]][[fr:Kofun de Matsuokayama]][[ja:松岳山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11530088]] Wikidata name: Matsuokayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.569917|N|135.645722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun Group|WD=Q60986182}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|1=Matsuokayama Kofun|2=ja|3=松岳山古墳|4=fr|5=Kofun de Matsuokayama|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[:en:Jadeite|Jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[:en:Kawachi-Kokubu Station|Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of tumulus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Inside burial chamber File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-1.jpg|Southern standing monolith File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-2.jpg|Northern standing monolith 松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-1.JPG|Haniwa </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/?doc_id=5197 Kashiwara City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun]][[en:Matsuokayama Kofun]][[fr:Kofun de Matsuokayama]][[ja:松岳山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11530088]] Wikidata name: Matsuokayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun located in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsuokayama Kofun | native_name =松岳山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsuokayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.569917|N|135.645722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun Group|WD=Q60986182}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Matsuokayama Kofun'''|松岳山古墳|1=Matsuokayama Kofun|2=ja|3=松岳山古墳|4=fr|5=Kofun de Matsuokayama|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kokubu-ichiba neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210630 |title=松岳山古墳|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The tumulus is the largest of the {{nihongo|Matsudakeyama Kofun Cluster|松岳山古墳群||}}, which has produced many important archaeological finds.
==Overview== The Matsuokayama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of 60 meters, the highest point of the narrow hills of Mt. Matsuoka (Miyama) on the south bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] as it enters the Osaka Plain in the eastern part of Osaka Prefecture, and within the grounds of a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], the Kokubu Jinja. The tumulus has a total length of between 130 and 155 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of 72 meters and is orientated to the east. The mound was constructed by stacking thin plate-like stones. The mound was originally constructed in three or four tiers on the posterior circle and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of oval cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''; however, the covering earth has eroded away, leading the sarcophagus exposed and the stone slabs used in the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] scattered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> This sarcophagus is a combination of six stones, one each for the lid and base stones and four one each side. Two side stones are accompanied by a rope-hanging protrusions. The surface of the bottom stone is carved to fit the body of the burial person.
Antiquarians reported recovered [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s from the site in 1877 and some stone masonry was removed from the site in 1915, but the current location of these artifacts is now unknown. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] have been conducted in surveys have been conducted in 1955 and from 1984 to 1986. Despite the poor conditions the site, a number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been detected inside and outside the sarcophagus, including [[:en:Jadeite|Jadeite]] ''[[magatama]]'' and jadeite, jasper or glass beads, copper and iron swords, spears, axes and other items. From these artifacts and the unusual construction technique and style of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the end of the 4th century. Most of the recovered artifacts are currently stored at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]], but three elliptical cylindrical ''haniwa'' and two pot-shaped pottery have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kashiwara City, and are currently stored in the Kashiwara City Historical Museum.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 |title=松岳山古墳出土品|trans-title= Matsuokayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=Kashiwara City |accessdate=February 20, 2022}}</ref>
The Matsuokayama Kofun is located about a 14-drive from [[:en:Kawachi-Kokubu Station|Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: between 130 and 155 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter 16 meters high, 3-tiers or 4-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of tumulus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:Matsuokayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Inside burial chamber File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-1.jpg|Southern standing monolith File:Matsuokayama Kofun, risseki-2.jpg|Northern standing monolith 松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-1.JPG|Haniwa </gallery>
